free_text: Elastic scattering of 12 MeV deuterons on
even rare-earth nuclei
Evaporated targets of enriched isotopes in the form
of oxides supported by thin carbon backing were used.
The thickness of the targets was from 50 mu-m/cm2 to
200 mu-g/cm2. The isotopic enrichment of the targets
was better than 80% in all cases.
After analysis in a high-resolution
spectrograph, the elastically scattered deuterons were
detected in a 2 mm thick Si(Li) counter.
Experimental uncertainty, taking into
account the uncertainty 0.1 degr. in setting the
scattering angle, the minor variations in
the spectrograph solid angle due to variations in the
beam spot position and the small error in the
normalization procedure.
The cross sections were normalized under the assumption
that the elastic scattering at 15 degr. is pure
Rutherford scattering.
